nameMeanings.name: Prince
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: nickname from Middle English, Old French prince (from Latin princeps).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Prinz ‘prince’, or its Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or some other Slavic cognate Princ, from princ ‘prince’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Primc: status name for one who lives in the house of his father-in-law, from a derivative of primiti ‘to accept, to take over’.

variants.name: Prinz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Matthias, Ulrich, Arno, Friedrich, Hans, Klaus, Mechthild.

German: nickname from Middle High German prinze ‘prince’, presumably denoting someone who behaved in a regal manner or who had won the title in some contest of skill. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Czechia.

nameMeanings.name: Adam
meanings: Hebrew: The Scripture name, in Hebrew, Adham, signifying 'man;' literally earthy, red earth; Rudolph, however, says the name in Ethiopic means ' to be fair, beautiful.' Hence the nurse-name Addy, little Adam.

nameMeanings.name: Baseline
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Donato, Marco, Marino.
Italian: altered form of Basilace, from the Greek personal name Basilakēs, a pet form of Basileios (see Basil ).

variants.name: Basil
meanings: From the Greek name Basileios ‘royal’ (a derivative of basileus ‘king’). This name was borne by St Basil the Great ( c.330–379 ), bishop of Caesarea, a theologian regarded as one of the Fathers of the Eastern Church. It was also the name of several early saints martyred in the East.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Adam Baseline Prince was born in 1429, in Nuneham Courtenay, Oxfordshire, England, his father, William Henry Prince, was 39 and his mother, Margaret, was 14. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1485, in Nuneham Courtenay, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56.

ko.lifeSummary: Adam Baseline Prince(이)가 1429년 영국 스퍼드실르 누니함 쿠르텐아이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Henry Prince(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret(은)는 14세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1485년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 누니함 쿠르텐아이에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.
